response.justification: Maria Rodriguez is a highly experienced full-stack and web developer with strong TypeScript/Node.js skills, but is fundamentally mismatched for this role. The position has two hard blockers: native-level Japanese proficiency (which Maria does not possess — her languages are English, Romanian, and basic German) and deep C/Linux kernel experience for embedded networking security. Maria's background is predominantly web, mobile, and cloud; her C exposure is limited to a student thesis and one brief IDE tooling role. There is no evidence of security domain knowledge (MACsec, 802.1X, captive portals), embedded firmware development, network switch internals, or Linux kernel modification — all of which are central to this position.
strengths: 15+ years of broad software development experience across embedded, desktop, web, and cloud environments demonstrates strong technical adaptability
Some embedded and hardware exposure at Altom Consulting (robotic arms, device orchestration tooling) and a thesis project using Linux, C++, and Jack audio
Strong software craftsmanship practices (TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development) and demonstrated ability to learn new stacks quickly
concerns: No Japanese language proficiency whatsoever — a stated hard requirement for this role
No experience with security technologies required by the role: MACsec, IEEE 802.1X, Captive Portal, MAC authentication, or Linux kernel vulnerability countermeasures
Primary stack is TypeScript/Node.js/DLang; C proficiency is minimal and not demonstrated in any UNIX systems or embedded networking context
